policy_statement: Strategy 1 (Pillar 1): Retrofit buildings.
Retrofit residential buildings with 4 or fewer units: retrofit 20% by 2030 and 50% by 2040, prioritizing low- or moderate-income households.
Retrofit 20% of total 5+ unit residential buildings by 2030.
Retrofit 20% of total industrial buildings by 2030.
Retrofit 90% of total City-owned and sister agency-owned buildings by 2035.
Strategy 2 (Pillar 1): Connect communities to renewable energy.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 1): Connect communities to renewable energy.
policy_statement: Install 5 megawatts of co-owned community solar projects by 2025.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 1): Connect communities to renewable energy.
policy_statement: Increase Chicago-based community renewables to 20 megawatts by 2025.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 1): Connect communities to renewable energy.
policy_statement: Increase community renewables subscriptions so that low-income and/or environmental justice low-income residents achieve 25% subscribed by 2030.
Strategy 1 (Pillar 2): Reduce waste and landfilling.
Introduce community-wide organic waste collection and decomposition by 2025.
Implement equitable waste source prevention strategies by 2030.
Divert 90% of commercial, industrial, and institutional waste by 2030.
Divert 75% of construction and demolition waste by 2030.
Enable building design for disassembly and reuse by 2035.
Divert 90% of residential waste by 2040.
Strategy 1 (Pillar 3): Make walking, biking, or transit viable options for all trips.

parent_statement: Strategy 1 (Pillar 3): Make walking, biking, or transit viable options for all trips.
policy_statement: Expand high-quality and low-stress on-street bikeways and off-street trails.

parent_statement: Strategy 1 (Pillar 3): Make walking, biking, or transit viable options for all trips.
policy_statement: Increase Divvy bikes and shared micromobility trips 30% by 2030.

parent_statement: Strategy 1 (Pillar 3): Make walking, biking, or transit viable options for all trips.
policy_statement: Enable Chicagoans to walk, bike, take transit, or use shared micromobility for 45% of all trips by 2040.
Strategy 2 (Pillar 3): Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 3): Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development.
policy_statement: Update land use policies to encourage sustainable development, accessibility, and street safety by 2023.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 3): Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development.
policy_statement: Expand use of commuter benefits by 2024.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 3): Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development.
policy_statement: Require transportation demand management plans for new development by 2025.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 3): Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development.
policy_statement: Update citywide car and bike parking requirements by 2025.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 3): Increase transit performance and encourage equitable transit-oriented development.
policy_statement: Increase CTA ridership 20% by 2030.
Strategy 3 (Pillar 3): Enable zero-emission transit and fleets.

parent_statement: Strategy 3 (Pillar 3): Enable zero-emission transit and fleets.
policy_statement: Enable electric freight loading docks at commercial and industrial buildings, addressing new buildings by 2025 and existing buildings by 2030.

parent_statement: Strategy 3 (Pillar 3): Enable zero-emission transit and fleets.
policy_statement: Support equitable electrification of ride-hail and taxi fleets by 2030.

parent_statement: Strategy 3 (Pillar 3): Enable zero-emission transit and fleets.
policy_statement: Enable 100% electrification of delivery fleets by 2035.

parent_statement: Strategy 3 (Pillar 3): Enable zero-emission transit and fleets.
policy_statement: Electrify 100% of the City's fleet by 2035.

parent_statement: Strategy 3 (Pillar 3): Enable zero-emission transit and fleets.
policy_statement: Achieve zero-emission transit fleets across Chicagoland by 2040.
Strategy 1 (Pillar 4): 100% clean renewable energy.
Aggregate 5,000 megawatts of clean renewable energy within a 250-mile radius of Chicago by 2030.
Install 30 megawatts of clean renewable energy projects on City property by 2030.
Achieve 100% clean renewable energy community-wide by 2035.
Strategy 2 (Pillar 4): Enable building and personal vehicle electrification.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 4): Enable building and personal vehicle electrification.
policy_statement: Enact policies that support electrified renovations and new construction by 2023.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 4): Enable building and personal vehicle electrification.
policy_statement: Electrify 30% of total existing residential buildings by 2035.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 4): Enable building and personal vehicle electrification.
policy_statement: Electrify 90% of total existing City-owned buildings by 2035.

parent_statement: Strategy 2 (Pillar 4): Enable building and personal vehicle electrification.
policy_statement: Enable 2,500 new public passenger electric vehicle charging stations by 2035, prioritizing low- and middle-income communities.
Strategy 3 (Pillar 4): Align building codes and standards with climate best practices.

parent_statement: Strategy 3 (Pillar 4): Align building codes and standards with climate best practices.
policy_statement: Strengthen policies that support installation of green roofs and walls, tree planting, and other vegetative cover by 2023.

parent_statement: Strategy 3 (Pillar 4): Align building codes and standards with climate best practices.
policy_statement: Enable net-zero-carbon construction by 2040.
Strategy 4 (Pillar 4): Decommission fossil power.
Develop a fossil-fuel plants transition strategy by 2024.
Strategy 5 (Pillar 4): Enable interconnection and storage.
Ensure 150 megawatts of energy storage by 2025.
Achieve 1,000 megawatts of new energy demand reduction capacity by 2030 and 3,000 megawatts by 2040.
Strategy 1 (Pillar 5): Collect relevant data.
Report energy burden by community area by 2023.
Develop a water and soil quality measurement and mitigation strategy by 2023.
Establish a robust outdoor air quality monitoring network by 2025.
Strategy 2 (Pillar 5): Enable data-driven decision-making.
Publish clean energy just transition metrics by 2022.
Integrate community resilience and climate justice criteria into department-level strategic planning and annual budget setting by 2023.
Develop a Heat Vulnerability Index and integrate it into planning and development, community safety, and public health planning processes beginning in 2023.
Publish citywide and community-level quality-of-life metrics on equity and sustainability by 2023, and report them every year.
Assess and optimize community-level emergency management strategies related to climate preparedness by 2024.
Strategy 3 (Pillar 5): Enable community resiliency.
Resource community-led climate infrastructure projects by 2022.
Integrate community resiliency strategies with the City's hazard mitigation planning by 2023.
City of Chicago aims to reduce city GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040 (using 2017 inventory levels as baseline).
Plant 75,000 trees over 5 years (Chicago Recovery Plan climate investment: Expand Canopy Coverage, $46 million).
